titleStmt.title: The Minister-Counselor of Embassy in China ( (Butterworth) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Minister-Counselor of Embassy in China ( Butterworth ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 418. Pending the receipt of a detailed report from Changchun on the capture by the Communists of Assistant Military Attachés Rigg and Collins (ReEmbtel 408), the Embassy decided not to make representations to Nanking Communist representatives since it was hoped that there might be circumstances which would facilitate their early release thru local action. It was considered undesirable, on the basis of present scanty information to involve YenanShensi location of Chinese Communist Party headquarters. in the case of which it was most probably completely unaware. Any reference to Yenan would almost certainly have provoked consequences likely to delay settlement.

This morning the Embassy heard on the radio the War Department’s announcement, the reasons for which are obscure. In these
circumstances, I took up the matter with Wang Ping-nan,Spokesman of the Chinese Communist Party delegation at Nanking. and asked him to request Yenan to issue instructions for the immediate release of Rigg and Collins. He agreed to communicate with Yenan forthwith.
Colonel Dau, Ex. Officer MA,Col. Frederick J. Dau, Assistant Military Attaché and Executive Officer in the Office of the Military Attaché. is flying to Changchun March 3.
